channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Got a bank transfer alert text? It might be a scam; here’s what to do
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips to safeguard your personal information as scammers become more sophisticated with scam texts and other methods.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This secret site lets you try DeepSeek on a trustworthy U.S. server
item.description: We need to talk about AI. Have you noticed it often just isn’t—well, very intelligent? Already, we’ve lived through years of AI hype. We’ve watched companies pitch AI as a great tool for writing boring corporate emails. We’ve seen it shoehorned into all kinds of places it doesn’t belong.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: iPad shoppers beware: One of the new models is not like the others
item.description: This week, Apple updated half of its iPad lineup. After updating the iPad Pro and iPad mini in 2024, the company has just unveiled a third-generation iPad Air and an eleventh-generation iPad.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX’s Starship explodes again, with wreckage seen from Florida
item.description: Nearly two months after an explosion sent flaming debris raining down on the Turks and Caicos, SpaceX launched another mammoth Starship rocket on Thursday, but lost contact minutes into the test flight as the spacecraft came tumbling down and broke apart.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Scientists date remains of an ancient child that resembles both humans and Neanderthals
item.description: Scientists have dated the skeleton of an ancient child that caused a stir when it was first discovered because it carries features from both humans and Neanderthals.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This is Amazon's first foray in servers, and certainly not the last: MicroTik franken-router is powered by the AWS Graviton 1 Arm CPU
item.description: MicroTik's RDS2216 data server has 16 ports and is powered by Amazon's Graviton 1 Arm CPU used on AWS.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Glydways wants to reinvent transit—no more than four passengers at a time
item.description: Management at the Bay Area transportation startup Glydways wants you to be clear about what the company is not: It may plan to move people in futuristic autonomous pods, but it’s not hyperloop-grade vaporware. And its funding by big-name Silicon Valley investors does not make it a ride for the 1%.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: US military's mini space shuttle returns to Earth after orbiting for 434 days on a secret mission
item.description: The U.S. military's classified mini space shuttle has returned to Earth after circling the world for 434 days.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Disco Elysium successor XXX Nightshift nets some serious musical talent
item.description: Disco Elysium successor XXX Nightshift will feature a soundtrack led by songwriter and music producer John Cunningham.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: IT execs need to embrace a new role: myth-buster
item.description: One of the most dangerous trends arising from the combination of access to generative AI (genAI) tools, the cloud, and mobile apps is how easy it is for a line-of-business chief to do an end-run around IT and deploy all manner of things.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why weird JD Vance memes have taken over the internet
item.description: Ironically enough, a divisive moment in the Oval Office last weekend seems to have brought the entire internet together.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.98 shines on GitHub Copilot
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.98 has arrived, featuring enhancements to the GitHub Copilot AI coding assistant, including previews of Copilot Next Edit Suggestions, Copilot agent mode improvements, and Copilot Edits support for notebook files.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AI chatbots infected with Russian disinformation: Study
item.description: The world’s most popular artificial intelligence (AI) chatbots are infected with Russian disinformation, according to a study published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation
item.description: All components of a zero trust architecture must generate data that is observable, available and able to be correlated within a unified data plane. The post Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Senate Democrat slams Trump's 'grift' meme coins
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) slammed President Trump for his launch of a meme coin in January, suggesting it might be the "biggest White House corruption in a century.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Rapid Evolution of Server Technology: Trends and Insights
item.description: The pace of change around server technology is advancing considerably, driven by hyperscalers but spilling over into the on-premises world as well.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How Audiomack became an unlikely Spotify competitor
item.description: Kendrick Lamar. Drake. Lady Gaga. The charts of music streaming services pretty much all look the same these days, with familiar names dominating the top spots—except on up-and-coming Spotify competitor Audiomack. The current No.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI driving a 165% rise in data center power demand by 2030
item.description: Global power demand from data centers will increase 50% by 2027 and by as much as 165% by the end of the decade compared with 2023, according to a recent report from Goldman Sachs Research The industry is shrugging off the potential of DeepSeek – which claimed large-scale AI modeling capabilities fr
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why you should use dependency injection
item.description: A few weeks ago, I wrote about how a good software team will defer decisions as long as possible. Smart teams will design and build systems that don’t lock them into any particular implementation until it is utterly necessary.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SolarWinds buys Squadcast to speed incident response
item.description: Observability provider SolarWinds announced this week it had signed an agreement to acquire San Francisco-based Squadcast and its incident response technology for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tariffs for now won’t impact IT organizations
item.description: The impact of tariffs by the Trump Administration on semiconductors from Taiwan is so far not cause for alarm, according to one analyst.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Improving intelligent document processing with generative AI
item.description: One of the earliest digital transformation drivers was digitalization, in which organizations converted paper-based processes to digitized workflows. In ideal circumstances, paper documents became web or mobile forms, and workflows replaced handoffs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The 2025 Terminator? Lab-grown muscle brings biohybrid robot hand to life
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how lab-grown muscles power a biohybrid hand for scalable prosthetics and research.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: President Trump announced Sunday that the U.S. would be creating a crypto reserve, to boost the growing industry.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation
